# Taylor (NotGreg) + Hannah — February 19, 2026

**Type:** External / Exploratory Relationship
**Participants:** Dax, Taylor, Hannah (Taylor's 19-year-old sister and 50% business partner — joined partway through)

---

## Overview

A follow-up conversation that went deeper on both sides. Taylor brought in Hannah, his sister and business partner. The main thrust from Taylor was consulting feedback on DD's business model — his read is that DD gives too much away for free and retention is the core problem. Dax shared more operational context about where DD is and where it's going. Both sides outlined what they could offer each other. The PA connection was a notable moment.

---

## Taylor's Consulting Take on DD

Taylor's primary observation: 8,000 members, ~50 paying is a value proposition problem — free content removes urgency to pay. His recommendation: paywall more, and explicitly charge for the hands-on engagement Dax provides (teaching members, answering questions, building their confidence). He referenced Gary Vee's point that people will pay just to walk alongside someone they trust — that's what Dax is already doing for free.

---

## What Was Discussed

**DD operational context Dax shared:**
- Raising price to $50/month imminently — hasn't changed from $15 in 3 years
- 80% of traffic organic — Fliff Discord SEO + listing sites (Discadia, Discord.me, Hive Index)
- ~65% accuracy on sent alerts in 2025, targeting 70% this season
- Building EV filters to cut 2,000 daily alerts to 5–10/sport — nearly complete
- Staff trimmed from 10–12 to 6; commission-only, independent contractors, under W9 threshold
- Developer Zach: shifted from factory job to full-time software role at his employer — less time for DD
- Dax learning Claude/coding to handle more of the codebase himself

**Dax's fintech internship:**
"Savings account for gamblers" — users deposit, company gives sports picks, return is a % if picks win, 0% if they don't. Still defining value proposition. Taylor was interested and flagged his dev partners (Sumer Labs, Collegeville PA) as a potential connection for the company.

**PA connection:**
Taylor worked at Collegeville Best Buy 2014–2015. Dax grew up in Collegeville — parents still live there. Taylor plans to sublet in Collegeville area this year for client work. Both noted the coincidence.

**Hannah's role:**
Taylor's 19-year-old sister. He gave her 50% of his income/business this year. She sat in and listened — minimal participation but Taylor uses her presence as a signal he's treating this as a real business relationship.

---

## What Each Side Offered

**Taylor offered:**
- Free business/banking counsel ongoing (no cost, in exchange for Discord help)
- Digital bank (Wells Fargo-affiliated, no brick+mortar): 1% cash back debit, instant payroll deposits, zero-interest short-term loans against upcoming direct deposits — will send links
- Organic member growth — sending degenerate gambler friends to DD
- Staff referrals — knowledgeable bettor candidates for DD
- Fintech intro — connect Sumer Labs to Dax's internship company

**Dax offered:**
- Discord walkthrough — screen share at leisure, no hard timeline
- Google Calendar link for fintech internship interview/beta tester conversation

---

## Follow-Up Plan

- Stay in touch via Discord DMs or text; Taylor prefers text, Discord notifications off
- Taylor to send business banking links for Dax to review independently
- Dax to send Taylor a Google Calendar link for fintech interview
- Discord walkthrough at Dax's leisure
